scene_count_explanation.question: Why only 2 weeks (4 scenes) of 2026 data?
scene_count_explanation.short_answer: We have ALL the scenes that exist in the archive. The STAC catalog has a 10-14 day processing lag — scenes from April 14-25 do not exist in the database yet. We are not missing data. The data has not been published by ESA/Microsoft yet.

detail.why_lag_exists: Sentinel-2 raw data → ESA ground station → atmospheric correction processing → Microsoft Planetary Computer ingestion → STAC API availability. This chain takes 10-14 days. It is not a VIGIL limitation — it is the global data supply chain.
detail.sentinel2_revisit_cycle_days: 3-5 (3 satellites now: S2A, S2B, S2C confirmed in our data)

scenes_expected_but_not_yet_published.reason: S2A pass — not yet in STAC
S2B pass — not yet in STAC
S2C pass — not yet in STAC
S2A pass — not yet in STAC

detail.clarification: We used 4 scenes (not 2). They fall into 2 weekly bins: week 0 (Apr 1-7: 2 scenes) and week 1 (Apr 8-14: 2 scenes). The pipeline aggregates into weekly averages, which is why it shows '2 weeks' — not '2 scenes'.
detail.when_will_more_appear: Re-run the pipeline on May 1-3 and you will have 4-6 more scenes covering April 16-28. By May 10, weeks 2-3 will be fully populated. The cache means only new scenes are downloaded.
detail.fix_for_faster_data: Switch to Google Earth Engine (GEE) — their Sentinel-2 latency is 1-3 days instead of 10-14. This is the next infrastructure upgrade. Current pipeline uses Microsoft Planetary Computer which is free but slower.

financial_model_v2.model_philosophy: We report cost savings in ₹ only for irrigation and fertilizer — inputs we can calculate directly from satellite and weather data. For yield saved, we report kg/acre only. The farmer multiplies by their own selling price. We never assume a grape price.

section_1_direct_cost_savings.title: Money saved directly — irrigation and fertilizer
section_1_direct_cost_savings.note: These are costs the farmer ALREADY pays. VIGIL reduces them. No price assumption needed.

irrigation_savings.how_we_calculate: We compare VIGIL-scheduled irrigation (ET0 × crop coefficient × area) against the typical fixed-schedule irrigation (same daily dose regardless of weather). On cool/cloudy days: fixed schedule over-irrigates. On 40°C days: it under-irrigates. VIGIL adjusts every 6 days based on actual ET0 from Open-Meteo.
vigil_corrects: Over-irrigation on moderate days (saves water + electricity + root disease risk)
Under-irrigation on 38-40°C days (saves yield via preventing berry stress drop)

irrigation_cost_benchmark_rs_per_acre_pe.source: Maharashtra drip irrigation electricity cost — ₹5-8/unit, 1.5kW pump, 4-6 hours/day

fertilizer_savings.how_we_calculate: NDRE (red-edge index) detects chlorophyll and nitrogen status in the canopy 2-3 weeks before the farmer can see yellowing with the naked eye. Current NDRE for this farm: 0.134. When NDRE drops >10% below baseline, targeted nitrogen application is recommended — stopping blanket weekly applications in non-deficient zones.
fertilizer_savings.ndre_status: Within normal range — no nitrogen deficiency currently detected

fertilizer_cost_benchmark_rs_per_acre_pe.source: Maharashtra viticulture extension estimates — NPK + micronutrients for grapes

fertilizer_savings.savings_mechanism: Targeted application only to deficient zones; skip areas at adequate levels

section_2_yield_saved_in_kg.title: Grapes saved from rot and waste — reported in kg/acre only
section_2_yield_saved_in_kg.note: Multiply by your own selling price (₹ per kg) to get your money saved. We do not assume a price because it changes every year and every mandi.
section_2_yield_saved_in_kg.note_on_yield_assumption: 8,000 kg/acre is the mid-range for managed table grape vineyards in Pune district. Replace with your actual farm yield if known — all percentages still apply.

loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.loss_type: Berry drop from water stress during bunch formation
loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.what_happens_without_vigil: When soil moisture drops below the critical threshold during bunch initiation (typically 4-6 weeks before harvest), the vine aborts entire bunches. Dropped bunches rot on the ground — zero value. A fixed irrigation schedule misses the 3-4 day window when intervention prevents the drop.
loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.what_vigil_does: VIGIL flags ET0 × Kc deficit 6 days in advance (weather forecast layer). Farmer irrigates 2 days BEFORE the stress threshold is crossed. Bunches stay on the vine.
loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.evidence_basis: Dry et al. 2001 (Australian viticulture); Intrigliolo & Castel 2010 (Tempranillo); FAO irrigation scheduling manual
loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.confidence: MODERATE — mechanism well-established; percentage is for comparable cultivar-climates

loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.loss_type: Berry splitting and rot from heat events (Botrytis entry)
loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.what_happens_without_vigil: At >38°C for 2+ consecutive days, rapid water loss causes osmotic pressure changes in developing berries → skin micro-cracks → Botrytis cinerea (grey mold) enters through crack → whole cluster can become unsellable within 48-72 hours post-crack.
loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.what_vigil_does: The 6-day weather forecast in the report gives 2-5 days warning before a heat event. Pre-event irrigation keeps turgor pressure stable → fewer cracks → less Botrytis entry. Today's alert (40.3°C forecast for April 26) is exactly this mechanism.
loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.evidence_basis: Greer & Weedon 2012 (heat stress in Vitis); Mira de Orduña 2010 (climate impacts on wine grapes)
loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.confidence: MODERATE — heat crack mechanism is established; Botrytis linkage is well-documented for humid conditions

loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.loss_type: Undersized or pale berries from undetected nitrogen deficiency
loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.what_happens_without_vigil: Nitrogen deficiency causes reduced chlorophyll → small berries, pale colour, low brix (sugar). By the time the farmer sees yellowing leaves, the berry has already lost 3-4 weeks of development. Undersized berries are rejected at the mandi or sold at 30-50% price discount.
loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.what_vigil_does: NDRE detects chlorophyll decline 14-21 days before visual symptoms. Report flags 'apply nitrogen fertilizer within 7 days' before berry sizing is impacted. Farmer catches and corrects the deficiency during the window when it still matters.
loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.note: This does not save grapes from rotting — it saves berries from being REJECTED. Kg saved = kg that would have been below market grade, now converted to marketable grade.
loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.evidence_basis: Conradie 1992 (nitrogen effects on Vitis vinifera yield); Keller 2010 (The Science of Grapevines)
loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.confidence: INDICATIVE — NDRE-to-yield link is established but the conversion rate varies by variety and soil type

loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.loss_type: Fungal disease (downy mildew / powdery mildew) early-detection prevention
loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.what_happens_without_vigil: Monsoon onset (June in Pune) causes rapid humidity spikes. Downy mildew (Plasmopara viticola) can destroy 20-40% of unprotected bunches within 2 weeks of first infection. Farmers typically spray reactively — after they see the disease, which is 7-14 days after infection started.
loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.what_vigil_does: NDRE and composite stress score drop 10-14 days before visible mildew. Report flags 'begin preventive copper spray' BEFORE the infection spreads. Preventive spray at emergence vs reactive spray at spread = 1-2 spray cycles vs 4-6 spray cycles, and 10-15x less crop loss.
loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.evidence_basis: Lalancette et al. 1988; Gessler et al. 2011 (downy mildew epidemiology); Maharashtra state extension data on Pune grape losses
loss_mechanisms_we_prevent.confidence: MODERATE-HIGH — the spray timing benefit is well-established; our NDRE signal as an early proxy is the uncertain link

total_kg_saved_per_acre_per_season.note: Low + Mid + High ranges sum all four mechanisms

what_the_farmer_does_with_this.formula: Your money saved = kg_saved_per_acre × your actual selling price (₹/kg)
what_the_farmer_does_with_this.note: The kg_saved figure is VIGIL's claim. The ₹ conversion is the farmer's own calculation using their own mandi price.

what_farmer_gets_from_vigil: Direct cost savings (irrigation + fertilizer): ₹5,760 per acre per season — no price assumption, this is pump electricity and fertilizer bag bills
Yield saved from rot/waste/rejection: 1,680–3,760 kg per acre per season
Farmer multiplies saved kg × their own selling price to get yield-side savings

what_farmer_pays_vigil.model: 10% of documented direct cost savings + 1% of kg-saved (in ₹ at farmer's mandi price)

section_3_full_per_acre_summary.vigil_roi_statement: For every ₹1 the farmer pays VIGIL, they keep ₹9-60 in savings — depending on grape price and farm size. The exact number is confirmed after the first harvest season.
